title: JSON-schedule blackout calendar
description: Minimum-viable change-window blackout calendar for operators who do not maintain an iCal server. Defines a list of time-bounded blackout windows and optional warning threshold.

blackouts.description: List of blackout windows during which changes are not permitted.

start.description: ISO-8601 date-time string for when the blackout window begins. Accepts timezone-aware timestamps or naive timestamps interpreted via the top-level timezone field.

end.description: ISO-8601 date-time string for when the blackout window ends (exclusive). Accepts timezone-aware timestamps or naive timestamps interpreted via the top-level timezone field. Must be after start.

label.description: Optional human-readable name shown in hook deny-rationale text (e.g. 'Production freeze', 'CAB blackout').

warn_before_minutes.description: Minutes before an upcoming blackout start at which to emit a warning decision instead of allowed. Defaults to 60 if absent.

timezone.description: IANA timezone name (e.g. 'America/Chicago') used to interpret naive timestamps in blackout entries. If absent, naive timestamps are treated as UTC.
